title: pilosella caespitosa subsp. sudetorum
intro: Pilosella caespitosa subsp. sudetorum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, Asteraceae, that is native to Central Europe. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as a natural remedy for skin conditions. Pilosella caespitosa subsp. sudetorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
